implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Executes an HTTP GET request to the JSONPlaceholder users endpoint, converts the response to CSV format, and writes the CSV file to persistent storage.
implementation.name: main
implementation.pseudo_code:
CALL fetch(USERS_URL)
IF response not OK THEN THROW error
PARSE response JSON into users array
DEFINE CSV headers for selected fields
FOR EACH user IN users:
    CONVERT user fields to CSV row using userToRow
JOIN headers and rows into full CSV string
ENSURE data directory exists
WRITE CSV string to data/users.csv
PRINT success message

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Maps a user object to an array of CSV-safe strings for each predefined column. Used by main() to generate the CSV rows.
implementation.name: userToRow
implementation.pseudo_code:
RETURN array[
    id,
    escaped name,
    escaped username,
    escaped email,
    escaped address.street,
    escaped address.city,
    escaped address.zipcode,
    escaped phone,
    escaped website
]

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Ensures text fields containing special characters are properly escaped before being written into the CSV file.
implementation.name: escapeCsvField
implementation.pseudo_code:
CONVERT input to string
IF string contains comma, quote, or newline:
    RETURN string wrapped in quotes with internal quotes doubled
ELSE
    RETURN string

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Provides access to the saved users.csv file by reading it, converting it back to JSON, and returning it over HTTP.
implementation.name: GET /api/users
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF users.csv does not exist:
    RETURN 404 with error JSON
READ users.csv
PARSE CSV into JSON array using csvToJson
RETURN JSON array

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Converts the generated CSV file back into structured JSON rows for the /api/users endpoint.
implementation.name: csvToJson
implementation.pseudo_code:
SPLIT CSV into non-empty lines
IF less than 2 lines THEN RETURN empty array
PARSE header line into header list
FOR EACH row line:
    PARSE row into values
    MAP values to header keys
RETURN array of row objects

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Parses a single CSV line with support for quoted fields. Used by csvToJson to reconstruct JSON objects from the saved CSV.
implementation.name: parseCsvLine
implementation.pseudo_code:
INITIALIZE result array, current string, inQuotes flag
FOR each character in line:
    IF quote:
        TOGGLE inQuotes or append escaped quote
    ELSE IF comma and not inQuotes:
        PUSH current to result and reset current
    ELSE
        APPEND character to current
PUSH final field
RETURN result

missing_implementations: CSV file contains columns corresponding to each field in the user JSON object (e.g., id, name, username, email, etc.).
CSV column names are identical to the field names in the JSON object.
No user data is omitted or transformed; all fields from the API response are included.
before saving information check filter for data with location as Boone

If the API request fails or the response is empty, the system does not create or modify the CSV file and signals an error. (Partially implemented: fetch failure throws an error, but empty-response handling still writes an empty CSV.)
